<pre style="width=97%;">
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># From NaCl Crystallisation Pathways; Aaron R. Finney and Matteo Salvalaglio</span>
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># Plumed driver file to post-process MD and well-tempered metadynamics simulations</span>

<br/><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># Fixed atom at the centre of the simulation box</span>
<span id="data/driver.datdefp_short"><b name="data/driver.datp" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datp","data/driver.datp","violet")'>p</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datp">The FIXEDATOM action with label <b>p</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">p</td><td width="5%"><font color="violet">atoms</font></td><td>virtual atom calculated by FIXEDATOM action</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">FIXEDATOM<div class="right">Add a virtual atom in a fixed position. This action has <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datdefp");'>hidden defaults</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_f_i_x_e_d_a_t_o_m.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">AT<div class="right">coordinates of the virtual atom<i></i></div></div>=2.,2.,2
</span><span id="data/driver.datdefp_long" style="display:none;"><b name="data/driver.datp" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datp","data/driver.datp","violet")'>p</b>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">FIXEDATOM<div class="right">Add a virtual atom in a fixed position. This action uses the <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datdefp");'>defaults shown here</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_f_i_x_e_d_a_t_o_m.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">AT<div class="right">coordinates of the virtual atom<i></i></div></div>=2.,2.,2  <div class="tooltip">SET_MASS<div class="right"> mass of the virtual atom<i></i></div></div>=1 <div class="tooltip">SET_CHARGE<div class="right"> charge of the virtual atom<i></i></div></div>=0
</span><br/><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># DEfine the ion groups</span>
<b name="data/driver.datgna" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datgna","data/driver.datgna","violet")'>gna</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datgna">The GROUP action with label <b>gna</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">gna</td><td width="5%"><font color="violet">atoms</font></td><td>indices of atoms specified in GROUP</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">GROUP<div class="right">Define a group of atoms so that a particular list of atoms can be referenced with a single label in definitions of CVs or virtual atoms. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_g_r_o_u_p.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOMS<div class="right">the numerical indexes for the set of atoms in the group<i></i></div></div>=6151-6520
<b name="data/driver.datgcl" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datgcl","data/driver.datgcl","violet")'>gcl</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datgcl">The GROUP action with label <b>gcl</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">gcl</td><td width="5%"><font color="violet">atoms</font></td><td>indices of atoms specified in GROUP</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">GROUP<div class="right">Define a group of atoms so that a particular list of atoms can be referenced with a single label in definitions of CVs or virtual atoms. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_g_r_o_u_p.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOMS<div class="right">the numerical indexes for the set of atoms in the group<i></i></div></div>=6521-6890
<b name="data/driver.datgions" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datgions","data/driver.datgions","violet")'>gions</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datgions">The GROUP action with label <b>gions</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">gions</td><td width="5%"><font color="violet">atoms</font></td><td>indices of atoms specified in GROUP</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">GROUP<div class="right">Define a group of atoms so that a particular list of atoms can be referenced with a single label in definitions of CVs or virtual atoms. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_g_r_o_u_p.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOMS<div class="right">the numerical indexes for the set of atoms in the group<i></i></div></div>=6151-6890
<br/><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># Measure coordination in the biased sphere </span>
<span id="data/driver.datcn0_short"><b name="data/driver.datcn0" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn0","data/driver.datcn0_shortcut","blue")'>cn0</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn0_shortcut">The COORDINATIONNUMBER action with label <b>cn0</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn0</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the value calculated by this action</td></tr></table></span>:  <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">COORDINATIONNUMBER<div class="right">Calculate the coordination numbers of atoms so that you can then calculate functions of the distribution of This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datcn0");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_o_r_d_i_n_a_t_i_o_n_n_u_m_b_e_r.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESA<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgna">gna</b> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESB<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgcl">gcl</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">the switching function that it used in the construction of the contact matrix<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} 
</span><span id="data/driver.datcn0_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datcn0")'># cn0:  COORDINATIONNUMBER SPECIESA=gna SPECIESB=gcl SWITCH={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} </span>
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># as follows (Click the red comment above to revert to the short version of the input):</span>
<b name="data/driver.datcn0_grp" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn0_grp","data/driver.datcn0_grp","violet")'>cn0_grp</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn0_grp">The GROUP action with label <b>cn0_grp</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn0_grp</td><td width="5%"><font color="violet">atoms</font></td><td>indices of atoms specified in GROUP</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">GROUP<div class="right">Define a group of atoms so that a particular list of atoms can be referenced with a single label in definitions of CVs or virtual atoms. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_g_r_o_u_p.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOMS<div class="right">the numerical indexes for the set of atoms in the group<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgna">gna</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcn0_mat" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn0_mat","data/driver.datcn0_mat","red")'>cn0_mat</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn0_mat">The CONTACT_MATRIX action with label <b>cn0_mat</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn0_mat</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>a matrix containing the weights for the bonds between each pair of atoms</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CONTACT_MATRIX<div class="right">Adjacency matrix in which two atoms are adjacent if they are within a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_n_t_a_c_t__m_a_t_r_i_x.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">specify the switching function to use between two sets of indistinguishable atoms<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} 
<b name="data/driver.datcn0_ones" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn0_ones","data/driver.datcn0_ones","blue")'>cn0_ones</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn0_ones">The CONSTANT action with label <b>cn0_ones</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn0_ones</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the constant value that was read from the plumed input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ONES<div class="right">Create a constant vector with all elements equal to one <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_o_n_e_s.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SIZE<div class="right">the number of ones that you would like to create<i></i></div></div>=370
<b name="data/driver.datcn0" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn0","data/driver.datcn0","blue")'>cn0</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn0">The MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT action with label <b>cn0</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn0</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector that is obtained by taking the product between the matrix and the vector that were input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT<div class="right">Calculate the product of the matrix and the vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_a_t_r_i_x__v_e_c_t_o_r__p_r_o_d_u_c_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn0_mat">cn0_mat</b>,<b name="data/driver.datcn0_ones">cn0_ones</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcn0_caverage" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn0_caverage","data/driver.datcn0_caverage","black")'>cn0_caverage</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn0_caverage">The MEAN action with label <b>cn0_caverage</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn0_caverage</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the mean of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MEAN<div class="right">Calculate the arithmetic mean of the elements in a vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_e_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn0">cn0</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<span style="color:blue"># --- End of included input --- </span></span><span id="data/driver.datcn1_short"><b name="data/driver.datcn1" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn1","data/driver.datcn1_shortcut","blue")'>cn1</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn1_shortcut">The COORDINATIONNUMBER action with label <b>cn1</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn1</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the value calculated by this action</td></tr></table></span>:  <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">COORDINATIONNUMBER<div class="right">Calculate the coordination numbers of atoms so that you can then calculate functions of the distribution of This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datcn1");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_o_r_d_i_n_a_t_i_o_n_n_u_m_b_e_r.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESA<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgcl">gcl</b> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESB<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgna">gna</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">the switching function that it used in the construction of the contact matrix<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0}
</span><span id="data/driver.datcn1_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datcn1")'># cn1:  COORDINATIONNUMBER SPECIESA=gcl SPECIESB=gna SWITCH={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0}</span>
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># as follows (Click the red comment above to revert to the short version of the input):</span>
<b name="data/driver.datcn1_grp" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn1_grp","data/driver.datcn1_grp","violet")'>cn1_grp</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn1_grp">The GROUP action with label <b>cn1_grp</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn1_grp</td><td width="5%"><font color="violet">atoms</font></td><td>indices of atoms specified in GROUP</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">GROUP<div class="right">Define a group of atoms so that a particular list of atoms can be referenced with a single label in definitions of CVs or virtual atoms. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_g_r_o_u_p.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOMS<div class="right">the numerical indexes for the set of atoms in the group<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgcl">gcl</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcn1_mat" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn1_mat","data/driver.datcn1_mat","red")'>cn1_mat</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn1_mat">The CONTACT_MATRIX action with label <b>cn1_mat</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn1_mat</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>a matrix containing the weights for the bonds between each pair of atoms</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CONTACT_MATRIX<div class="right">Adjacency matrix in which two atoms are adjacent if they are within a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_n_t_a_c_t__m_a_t_r_i_x.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">specify the switching function to use between two sets of indistinguishable atoms<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} 
<b name="data/driver.datcn1_ones" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn1_ones","data/driver.datcn1_ones","blue")'>cn1_ones</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn1_ones">The CONSTANT action with label <b>cn1_ones</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn1_ones</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the constant value that was read from the plumed input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ONES<div class="right">Create a constant vector with all elements equal to one <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_o_n_e_s.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SIZE<div class="right">the number of ones that you would like to create<i></i></div></div>=370
<b name="data/driver.datcn1" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn1","data/driver.datcn1","blue")'>cn1</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn1">The MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT action with label <b>cn1</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn1</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector that is obtained by taking the product between the matrix and the vector that were input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT<div class="right">Calculate the product of the matrix and the vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_a_t_r_i_x__v_e_c_t_o_r__p_r_o_d_u_c_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn1_mat">cn1_mat</b>,<b name="data/driver.datcn1_ones">cn1_ones</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcn1_caverage" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn1_caverage","data/driver.datcn1_caverage","black")'>cn1_caverage</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn1_caverage">The MEAN action with label <b>cn1_caverage</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn1_caverage</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the mean of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MEAN<div class="right">Calculate the arithmetic mean of the elements in a vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_e_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn1">cn1</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<span style="color:blue"># --- End of included input --- </span></span><br/><span id="data/driver.datsphere0_short"><b name="data/driver.datsphere0" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datsphere0","data/driver.datsphere0_shortcut","blue")'>sphere0</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datsphere0_shortcut">The INSPHERE action with label <b>sphere0</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">sphere0_morethan</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the number of cvs in the region of interest that are more than a certain threshold</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">INSPHERE<div class="right">This quantity can be used to calculate functions of the distribution of collective variables for the atoms that lie in a particular, user-specified part of of the cell. This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datsphere0");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_i_n_s_p_h_e_r_e.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOM<div class="right">the atom whose vicinity we are interested in examining<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datp">p</b> <div class="tooltip">DATA<div class="right">the label of an action that calculates multicolvars<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn0">cn0</b> <div class="tooltip">RADIUS<div class="right">the switching function that tells us the extent of the sphereical region of interest<i></i></div></div>={TANH R_0=1.5}  <div class="tooltip">MORE_THAN<div class="right">calcualte the number of colvars that are inside the region of interest and that are greater that a certain threshold<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=5.0}
</span><span id="data/driver.datsphere0_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datsphere0")'># sphere0: INSPHERE ATOM=p DATA=cn0 RADIUS={TANH R_0=1.5}  MORE_THAN={RATIONAL R_0=5.0}</span>
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># as follows (Click the red comment above to revert to the short version of the input):</span>
<b name="data/driver.datsphere0_grp" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datsphere0_grp","data/driver.datsphere0_grp","violet")'>sphere0_grp</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datsphere0_grp">The GROUP action with label <b>sphere0_grp</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">sphere0_grp</td><td width="5%"><font color="violet">atoms</font></td><td>indices of atoms specified in GROUP</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">GROUP<div class="right">Define a group of atoms so that a particular list of atoms can be referenced with a single label in definitions of CVs or virtual atoms. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_g_r_o_u_p.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOMS<div class="right">the numerical indexes for the set of atoms in the group<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn0">cn0</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcn0_sphere0_mt" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn0_sphere0_mt","data/driver.datcn0_sphere0_mt","blue")'>cn0_sphere0_mt</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn0_sphere0_mt">The MORE_THAN action with label <b>cn0_sphere0_mt</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn0_sphere0_mt</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of a function that is one if the if the input is more than a threshold to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MORE_THAN<div class="right">Use a switching function to determine how many of the input variables are more than a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_o_r_e__t_h_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn0">cn0</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">This keyword is used if you want to employ an alternative to the continuous swiching function defined above<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=5.0} 
<b name="data/driver.datsphere0_mt" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datsphere0_mt","data/driver.datsphere0_mt","blue")'>sphere0_mt</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datsphere0_mt">The CUSTOM action with label <b>sphere0_mt</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">sphere0_mt</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of an arbitrary function to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CUSTOM<div class="right">Calculate a combination of variables using a custom expression. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_u_s_t_o_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn0_sphere0_mt">cn0_sphere0_mt</b>,sphere0 <div class="tooltip">FUNC<div class="right">the function you wish to evaluate<i></i></div></div>=x*y <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<b name="data/driver.datsphere0_morethan" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datsphere0_morethan","data/driver.datsphere0_morethan","black")'>sphere0_morethan</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datsphere0_morethan">The SUM action with label <b>sphere0_morethan</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">sphere0_morethan</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the sum of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">SUM<div class="right">Calculate the sum of the arguments <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_s_u_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datsphere0_mt">sphere0_mt</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<span style="color:blue"># --- End of included input --- </span></span><span id="data/driver.datsphere1_short"><b name="data/driver.datsphere1" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datsphere1","data/driver.datsphere1_shortcut","blue")'>sphere1</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datsphere1_shortcut">The INSPHERE action with label <b>sphere1</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">sphere1_morethan</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the number of cvs in the region of interest that are more than a certain threshold</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">INSPHERE<div class="right">This quantity can be used to calculate functions of the distribution of collective variables for the atoms that lie in a particular, user-specified part of of the cell. This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datsphere1");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_i_n_s_p_h_e_r_e.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOM<div class="right">the atom whose vicinity we are interested in examining<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datp">p</b> <div class="tooltip">DATA<div class="right">the label of an action that calculates multicolvars<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn1">cn1</b> <div class="tooltip">RADIUS<div class="right">the switching function that tells us the extent of the sphereical region of interest<i></i></div></div>={TANH R_0=1.5}  <div class="tooltip">MORE_THAN<div class="right">calcualte the number of colvars that are inside the region of interest and that are greater that a certain threshold<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=5.0}

<b name="data/driver.datcn1_sphere1_mt" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn1_sphere1_mt","data/driver.datcn1_sphere1_mt","blue")'>cn1_sphere1_mt</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn1_sphere1_mt">The MORE_THAN action with label <b>cn1_sphere1_mt</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn1_sphere1_mt</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of a function that is one if the if the input is more than a threshold to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MORE_THAN<div class="right">Use a switching function to determine how many of the input variables are more than a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_o_r_e__t_h_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn1">cn1</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">This keyword is used if you want to employ an alternative to the continuous swiching function defined above<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=5.0} 
<b name="data/driver.datsphere1_mt" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datsphere1_mt","data/driver.datsphere1_mt","blue")'>sphere1_mt</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datsphere1_mt">The CUSTOM action with label <b>sphere1_mt</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">sphere1_mt</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of an arbitrary function to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CUSTOM<div class="right">Calculate a combination of variables using a custom expression. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_u_s_t_o_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn1_sphere1_mt">cn1_sphere1_mt</b>,sphere1 <div class="tooltip">FUNC<div class="right">the function you wish to evaluate<i></i></div></div>=x*y <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<b name="data/driver.datsphere1_morethan" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datsphere1_morethan","data/driver.datsphere1_morethan","black")'>sphere1_morethan</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datsphere1_morethan">The SUM action with label <b>sphere1_morethan</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">sphere1_morethan</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the sum of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">SUM<div class="right">Calculate the sum of the arguments <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_s_u_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datsphere1_mt">sphere1_mt</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<span style="color:blue"># --- End of included input --- </span></span><br/><b name="data/driver.datncrysts" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datncrysts","data/driver.datncrysts","black")'>ncrysts</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datncrysts">The COMBINE action with label <b>ncrysts</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">ncrysts</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>a linear compbination</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">COMBINE<div class="right">Calculate a polynomial combination of a set of other variables. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_m_b_i_n_e.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=sphere0.morethan,sphere1.morethan <div class="tooltip">POWERS<div class="right"> the powers to which you are raising each of the arguments in your function<i></i></div></div>=1,1 <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO
<b name="data/driver.datvcrysts" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datvcrysts","data/driver.datvcrysts","black")'>vcrysts</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datvcrysts">The COMBINE action with label <b>vcrysts</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">vcrysts</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>a linear compbination</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">COMBINE<div class="right">Calculate a polynomial combination of a set of other variables. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_m_b_i_n_e.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datncrysts">ncrysts</b> <div class="tooltip">POWERS<div class="right"> the powers to which you are raising each of the arguments in your function<i></i></div></div>=0.333 <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO

<br/><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># Measure coordination throughout the box</span>
<span id="data/driver.datcn2_short"><b name="data/driver.datcn2" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn2","data/driver.datcn2_shortcut","blue")'>cn2</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn2_shortcut">The COORDINATIONNUMBER action with label <b>cn2</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn2</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the value calculated by this action</td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn2_morethan</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the number of colvars that have a value more than a threshold</td></tr></table></span>:  <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">COORDINATIONNUMBER<div class="right">Calculate the coordination numbers of atoms so that you can then calculate functions of the distribution of This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datcn2");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_o_r_d_i_n_a_t_i_o_n_n_u_m_b_e_r.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESA<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgna">gna</b> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESB<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgcl">gcl</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">the switching function that it used in the construction of the contact matrix<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} <div class="tooltip">MORE_THAN<div class="right">calculate the number of variables that are more than a certain target value<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=5.0}
</span><span id="data/driver.datcn2_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datcn2")'># cn2:  COORDINATIONNUMBER SPECIESA=gna SPECIESB=gcl SWITCH={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} MORE_THAN={RATIONAL R_0=5.0}</span>
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># as follows (Click the red comment above to revert to the short version of the input):</span>
<b name="data/driver.datcn2_grp" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn2_grp","data/driver.datcn2_grp","violet")'>cn2_grp</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn2_grp">The GROUP action with label <b>cn2_grp</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn2_grp</td><td width="5%"><font color="violet">atoms</font></td><td>indices of atoms specified in GROUP</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">GROUP<div class="right">Define a group of atoms so that a particular list of atoms can be referenced with a single label in definitions of CVs or virtual atoms. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_g_r_o_u_p.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOMS<div class="right">the numerical indexes for the set of atoms in the group<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgna">gna</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcn2_mat" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn2_mat","data/driver.datcn2_mat","red")'>cn2_mat</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn2_mat">The CONTACT_MATRIX action with label <b>cn2_mat</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn2_mat</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>a matrix containing the weights for the bonds between each pair of atoms</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CONTACT_MATRIX<div class="right">Adjacency matrix in which two atoms are adjacent if they are within a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_n_t_a_c_t__m_a_t_r_i_x.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">specify the switching function to use between two sets of indistinguishable atoms<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} 
<b name="data/driver.datcn2_ones" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn2_ones","data/driver.datcn2_ones","blue")'>cn2_ones</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn2_ones">The CONSTANT action with label <b>cn2_ones</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn2_ones</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the constant value that was read from the plumed input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ONES<div class="right">Create a constant vector with all elements equal to one <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_o_n_e_s.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SIZE<div class="right">the number of ones that you would like to create<i></i></div></div>=370
<b name="data/driver.datcn2" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn2","data/driver.datcn2","blue")'>cn2</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn2">The MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT action with label <b>cn2</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn2</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector that is obtained by taking the product between the matrix and the vector that were input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT<div class="right">Calculate the product of the matrix and the vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_a_t_r_i_x__v_e_c_t_o_r__p_r_o_d_u_c_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn2_mat">cn2_mat</b>,<b name="data/driver.datcn2_ones">cn2_ones</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcn2_caverage" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn2_caverage","data/driver.datcn2_caverage","black")'>cn2_caverage</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn2_caverage">The MEAN action with label <b>cn2_caverage</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn2_caverage</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the mean of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MEAN<div class="right">Calculate the arithmetic mean of the elements in a vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_e_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn2">cn2</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<b name="data/driver.datcn2_mt" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn2_mt","data/driver.datcn2_mt","blue")'>cn2_mt</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn2_mt">The MORE_THAN action with label <b>cn2_mt</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn2_mt</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of a function that is one if the if the input is more than a threshold to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MORE_THAN<div class="right">Use a switching function to determine how many of the input variables are more than a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_o_r_e__t_h_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn2">cn2</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">This keyword is used if you want to employ an alternative to the continuous swiching function defined above<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=5.0} 
<b name="data/driver.datcn2_morethan" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn2_morethan","data/driver.datcn2_morethan","black")'>cn2_morethan</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn2_morethan">The SUM action with label <b>cn2_morethan</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn2_morethan</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the sum of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">SUM<div class="right">Calculate the sum of the arguments <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_s_u_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn2_mt">cn2_mt</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<span style="color:blue"># --- End of included input --- </span></span><span id="data/driver.datcn3_short"><b name="data/driver.datcn3" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn3","data/driver.datcn3_shortcut","blue")'>cn3</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn3_shortcut">The COORDINATIONNUMBER action with label <b>cn3</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn3</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the value calculated by this action</td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn3_morethan</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the number of colvars that have a value more than a threshold</td></tr></table></span>:  <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">COORDINATIONNUMBER<div class="right">Calculate the coordination numbers of atoms so that you can then calculate functions of the distribution of This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datcn3");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_o_r_d_i_n_a_t_i_o_n_n_u_m_b_e_r.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESA<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgcl">gcl</b> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESB<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgna">gna</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">the switching function that it used in the construction of the contact matrix<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} <div class="tooltip">MORE_THAN<div class="right">calculate the number of variables that are more than a certain target value<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=5.0}
</span><span id="data/driver.datcn3_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datcn3")'># cn3:  COORDINATIONNUMBER SPECIESA=gcl SPECIESB=gna SWITCH={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} MORE_THAN={RATIONAL R_0=5.0}</span>
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># as follows (Click the red comment above to revert to the short version of the input):</span>
<b name="data/driver.datcn3_grp" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn3_grp","data/driver.datcn3_grp","violet")'>cn3_grp</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn3_grp">The GROUP action with label <b>cn3_grp</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn3_grp</td><td width="5%"><font color="violet">atoms</font></td><td>indices of atoms specified in GROUP</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">GROUP<div class="right">Define a group of atoms so that a particular list of atoms can be referenced with a single label in definitions of CVs or virtual atoms. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_g_r_o_u_p.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOMS<div class="right">the numerical indexes for the set of atoms in the group<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgcl">gcl</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcn3_mat" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn3_mat","data/driver.datcn3_mat","red")'>cn3_mat</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn3_mat">The CONTACT_MATRIX action with label <b>cn3_mat</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn3_mat</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>a matrix containing the weights for the bonds between each pair of atoms</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CONTACT_MATRIX<div class="right">Adjacency matrix in which two atoms are adjacent if they are within a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_n_t_a_c_t__m_a_t_r_i_x.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">specify the switching function to use between two sets of indistinguishable atoms<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} 
<b name="data/driver.datcn3_ones" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn3_ones","data/driver.datcn3_ones","blue")'>cn3_ones</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn3_ones">The CONSTANT action with label <b>cn3_ones</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn3_ones</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the constant value that was read from the plumed input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ONES<div class="right">Create a constant vector with all elements equal to one <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_o_n_e_s.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SIZE<div class="right">the number of ones that you would like to create<i></i></div></div>=370
<b name="data/driver.datcn3" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn3","data/driver.datcn3","blue")'>cn3</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn3">The MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT action with label <b>cn3</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn3</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector that is obtained by taking the product between the matrix and the vector that were input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT<div class="right">Calculate the product of the matrix and the vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_a_t_r_i_x__v_e_c_t_o_r__p_r_o_d_u_c_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn3_mat">cn3_mat</b>,<b name="data/driver.datcn3_ones">cn3_ones</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcn3_caverage" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn3_caverage","data/driver.datcn3_caverage","black")'>cn3_caverage</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn3_caverage">The MEAN action with label <b>cn3_caverage</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn3_caverage</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the mean of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MEAN<div class="right">Calculate the arithmetic mean of the elements in a vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_e_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn3">cn3</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<b name="data/driver.datcn3_mt" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn3_mt","data/driver.datcn3_mt","blue")'>cn3_mt</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn3_mt">The MORE_THAN action with label <b>cn3_mt</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn3_mt</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of a function that is one if the if the input is more than a threshold to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MORE_THAN<div class="right">Use a switching function to determine how many of the input variables are more than a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_o_r_e__t_h_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn3">cn3</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">This keyword is used if you want to employ an alternative to the continuous swiching function defined above<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=5.0} 
<b name="data/driver.datcn3_morethan" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn3_morethan","data/driver.datcn3_morethan","black")'>cn3_morethan</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn3_morethan">The SUM action with label <b>cn3_morethan</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn3_morethan</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the sum of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">SUM<div class="right">Calculate the sum of the arguments <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_s_u_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn3_mt">cn3_mt</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<span style="color:blue"># --- End of included input --- </span></span><br/><b name="data/driver.datncrysta" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datncrysta","data/driver.datncrysta","black")'>ncrysta</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datncrysta">The COMBINE action with label <b>ncrysta</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">ncrysta</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>a linear compbination</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">COMBINE<div class="right">Calculate a polynomial combination of a set of other variables. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_m_b_i_n_e.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn2">cn2.morethan</b>,<b name="data/driver.datcn3">cn3.morethan</b> <div class="tooltip">POWERS<div class="right"> the powers to which you are raising each of the arguments in your function<i></i></div></div>=1,1 <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO
<b name="data/driver.datvcrysta" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datvcrysta","data/driver.datvcrysta","black")'>vcrysta</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datvcrysta">The COMBINE action with label <b>vcrysta</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">vcrysta</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>a linear compbination</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">COMBINE<div class="right">Calculate a polynomial combination of a set of other variables. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_m_b_i_n_e.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datncrysta">ncrysta</b> <div class="tooltip">POWERS<div class="right"> the powers to which you are raising each of the arguments in your function<i></i></div></div>=0.333 <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO

<br/><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># Measure coordination of crystalline ions in the biased sphere</span>
<span id="data/driver.datq60s_short"><b name="data/driver.datq60s" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datq60s","data/driver.datq60s_shortcut","blue")'>q60s</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datq60s_shortcut">The Q6 action with label <b>q60s</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">q60s</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the value calculated by this action</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">Q6<div class="right">Calculate sixth order Steinhardt parameters. This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datq60s");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_q6.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESA<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgna">gna</b> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESB<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgcl">gcl</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">the switching function that it used in the construction of the contact matrix<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} <div class="tooltip">LOWMEM<div class="right"> this flag does nothing and is present only to ensure back-compatibility<i></i></div></div>
</span><span id="data/driver.datq60s_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datq60s")'># q60s: Q6 SPECIESA=gna SPECIESB=gcl SWITCH={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} LOWMEM</span>
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># as follows (Click the red comment above to revert to the short version of the input):</span>
<b name="data/driver.datq60s_grp" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datq60s_grp","data/driver.datq60s_grp","violet")'>q60s_grp</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datq60s_grp">The GROUP action with label <b>q60s_grp</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">q60s_grp</td><td width="5%"><font color="violet">atoms</font></td><td>indices of atoms specified in GROUP</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">GROUP<div class="right">Define a group of atoms so that a particular list of atoms can be referenced with a single label in definitions of CVs or virtual atoms. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_g_r_o_u_p.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOMS<div class="right">the numerical indexes for the set of atoms in the group<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgna">gna</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datq60s_mat" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datq60s_mat","data/driver.datq60s_mat","red")'>q60s_mat</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datq60s_mat">The CONTACT_MATRIX action with label <b>q60s_mat</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">q60s_mat.w</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>a matrix containing the weights for the bonds between each pair of atoms</td></tr><tr><td width="5%">q60s_mat.x</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>the projection of the bond on the x axis</td></tr><tr><td width="5%">q60s_mat.y</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>the projection of the bond on the y axis</td></tr><tr><td width="5%">q60s_mat.z</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>the projection of the bond on the z axis</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CONTACT_MATRIX<div class="right">Adjacency matrix in which two atoms are adjacent if they are within a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_n_t_a_c_t__m_a_t_r_i_x.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">specify the switching function to use between two sets of indistinguishable atoms<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} <div class="tooltip">COMPONENTS<div class="right"> also calculate the components of the vector connecting the atoms in the contact matrix<i></i></div></div>

<b name="data/driver.datq60s" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datq60s","data/driver.datq60s","blue")'>q60s</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datq60s">The CUSTOM action with label <b>q60s</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">q60s</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of an arbitrary function to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CUSTOM<div class="right">Calculate a combination of variables using a custom expression. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_u_s_t_o_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datq60s_norm">q60s_norm</b>,<b name="data/driver.datq60s_denom">q60s_denom</b> <div class="tooltip">FUNC<div class="right">the function you wish to evaluate<i></i></div></div>=x/y <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<span style="color:blue"># --- End of included input --- </span></span><span id="data/driver.datq61s_short"><b name="data/driver.datq61s" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datq61s","data/driver.datq61s_shortcut","blue")'>q61s</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datq61s_shortcut">The Q6 action with label <b>q61s</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">q61s</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the value calculated by this action</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">Q6<div class="right">Calculate sixth order Steinhardt parameters. This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datq61s");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_q6.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESA<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgcl">gcl</b> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESB<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgna">gna</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">the switching function that it used in the construction of the contact matrix<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} <div class="tooltip">LOWMEM<div class="right"> this flag does nothing and is present only to ensure back-compatibility<i></i></div></div>
</span><span id="data/driver.datq61s_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datq61s")'># q61s: Q6 SPECIESA=gcl SPECIESB=gna SWITCH={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} LOWMEM</span>
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># as follows (Click the red comment above to revert to the short version of the input):</span>
<b name="data/driver.datq61s_grp" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datq61s_grp","data/driver.datq61s_grp","violet")'>q61s_grp</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datq61s_grp">The GROUP action with label <b>q61s_grp</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">q61s_grp</td><td width="5%"><font color="violet">atoms</font></td><td>indices of atoms specified in GROUP</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">GROUP<div class="right">Define a group of atoms so that a particular list of atoms can be referenced with a single label in definitions of CVs or virtual atoms. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_g_r_o_u_p.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOMS<div class="right">the numerical indexes for the set of atoms in the group<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgcl">gcl</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datq61s_mat" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datq61s_mat","data/driver.datq61s_mat","red")'>q61s_mat</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datq61s_mat">The CONTACT_MATRIX action with label <b>q61s_mat</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">q61s_mat.w</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>a matrix containing the weights for the bonds between each pair of atoms</td></tr><tr><td width="5%">q61s_mat.x</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>the projection of the bond on the x axis</td></tr><tr><td width="5%">q61s_mat.y</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>the projection of the bond on the y axis</td></tr><tr><td width="5%">q61s_mat.z</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>the projection of the bond on the z axis</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CONTACT_MATRIX<div class="right">Adjacency matrix in which two atoms are adjacent if they are within a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_n_t_a_c_t__m_a_t_r_i_x.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">specify the switching function to use between two sets of indistinguishable atoms<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} <div class="tooltip">COMPONENTS<div class="right"> also calculate the components of the vector connecting the atoms in the contact matrix<i></i></div></div>

<span style="color:blue"># --- End of included input --- </span></span><br/><span id="data/driver.datlq60s_short"><b name="data/driver.datlq60s" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datlq60s","data/driver.datlq60s_shortcut","blue")'>lq60s</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datlq60s_shortcut">The LOCAL_Q6 action with label <b>lq60s</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">lq60s</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the value calculated by this action</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">LOCAL_Q6<div class="right">Calculate the local degree of order around an atoms by taking the average dot product between the q_6 vector on the central atom and the q_6 vector on the atoms in the first coordination sphere. This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datlq60s");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_l_o_c_a_l__q6.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SPECIES<div class="right"><i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datq60s">q60s</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">This keyword is used if you want to employ an alternative to the continuous swiching function defined above<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} <div class="tooltip">LOWMEM<div class="right"> this flag does nothing and is present only to ensure back-compatibility<i></i></div></div>
</span><span id="data/driver.datlq60s_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datlq60s")'># lq60s: LOCAL_Q6 SPECIES=q60s SWITCH={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} LOWMEM</span>
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># as follows (Click the red comment above to revert to the short version of the input):</span>
<b name="data/driver.datlq60s_uvec" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datlq60s_uvec","data/driver.datlq60s_uvec","blue")'>lq60s_uvec</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datlq60s_uvec">The CONSTANT action with label <b>lq60s_uvec</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">lq60s_uvec</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the constant value that was read from the plumed input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ONES<div class="right">Create a constant vector with all elements equal to one <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_o_n_e_s.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SIZE<div class="right">the number of ones that you would like to create<i></i></div></div>=26
