<b name="data/driver.datlq60s" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datlq60s","data/driver.datlq60s","blue")'>lq60s</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datlq60s">The MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT action with label <b>lq60s</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">lq60s</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector that is obtained by taking the product between the matrix and the vector that were input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT<div class="right">Calculate the product of the matrix and the vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_a_t_r_i_x__v_e_c_t_o_r__p_r_o_d_u_c_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datlq60s_prod">lq60s_prod</b>,<b name="data/driver.datlq60s_ones">lq60s_ones</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datlq60s_denom" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datlq60s_denom","data/driver.datlq60s_denom","blue")'>lq60s_denom</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datlq60s_denom">The MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT action with label <b>lq60s_denom</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">lq60s_denom</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector that is obtained by taking the product between the matrix and the vector that were input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT<div class="right">Calculate the product of the matrix and the vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_a_t_r_i_x__v_e_c_t_o_r__p_r_o_d_u_c_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datlq60s_cmap">lq60s_cmap</b>,<b name="data/driver.datlq60s_ones">lq60s_ones</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datlq60s_av" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datlq60s_av","data/driver.datlq60s_av","blue")'>lq60s_av</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datlq60s_av">The CUSTOM action with label <b>lq60s_av</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">lq60s_av</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of an arbitrary function to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CUSTOM<div class="right">Calculate a combination of variables using a custom expression. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_u_s_t_o_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datlq60s">lq60s</b>,<b name="data/driver.datlq60s_denom">lq60s_denom</b> <div class="tooltip">FUNC<div class="right">the function you wish to evaluate<i></i></div></div>=x/y <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<span style="color:blue"># --- End of included input --- </span></span><span id="data/driver.datlq61s_short"><b name="data/driver.datlq61s" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datlq61s","data/driver.datlq61s_shortcut","blue")'>lq61s</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datlq61s_shortcut">The LOCAL_Q6 action with label <b>lq61s</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">lq61s</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the value calculated by this action</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">LOCAL_Q6<div class="right">Calculate the local degree of order around an atoms by taking the average dot product between the q_6 vector on the central atom and the q_6 vector on the atoms in the first coordination sphere. This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datlq61s");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_l_o_c_a_l__q6.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SPECIES<div class="right"><i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datq61s">q61s</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">This keyword is used if you want to employ an alternative to the continuous swiching function defined above<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} <div class="tooltip">LOWMEM<div class="right"> this flag does nothing and is present only to ensure back-compatibility<i></i></div></div>
</span><span id="data/driver.datlq61s_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datlq61s")'># lq61s: LOCAL_Q6 SPECIES=q61s SWITCH={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} LOWMEM</span>
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># as follows (Click the red comment above to revert to the short version of the input):</span>
<b name="data/driver.datlq61s_uvec" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datlq61s_uvec","data/driver.datlq61s_uvec","blue")'>lq61s_uvec</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datlq61s_uvec">The CONSTANT action with label <b>lq61s_uvec</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">lq61s_uvec</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the constant value that was read from the plumed input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ONES<div class="right">Create a constant vector with all elements equal to one <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_o_n_e_s.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SIZE<div class="right">the number of ones that you would like to create<i></i></div></div>=26
<b name="data/driver.datlq61s_grp" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datlq61s_grp","data/driver.datlq61s_grp","violet")'>lq61s_grp</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datlq61s_grp">The GROUP action with label <b>lq61s_grp</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">lq61s_grp</td><td width="5%"><font color="violet">atoms</font></td><td>indices of atoms specified in GROUP</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">GROUP<div class="right">Define a group of atoms so that a particular list of atoms can be referenced with a single label in definitions of CVs or virtual atoms. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_g_r_o_u_p.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOMS<div class="right">the numerical indexes for the set of atoms in the group<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datq61s">q61s</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datlq61s_nmat" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datlq61s_nmat","data/driver.datlq61s_nmat","red")'>lq61s_nmat</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datlq61s_nmat">The OUTER_PRODUCT action with label <b>lq61s_nmat</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">lq61s_nmat</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>a matrix containing the outer product of the two input vectors that was obtained using the function that was input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">OUTER_PRODUCT<div class="right">Calculate the outer product matrix of two vectors <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_o_u_t_e_r__p_r_o_d_u_c_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datq61s_norm">q61s_norm</b>,<b name="data/driver.datlq61s_uvec">lq61s_uvec</b>

<b name="data/driver.datlq61s_cmap" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datlq61s_cmap","data/driver.datlq61s_cmap","red")'>lq61s_cmap</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datlq61s_cmap">The CONTACT_MATRIX action with label <b>lq61s_cmap</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">lq61s_cmap</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>a matrix containing the weights for the bonds between each pair of atoms</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CONTACT_MATRIX<div class="right">Adjacency matrix in which two atoms are adjacent if they are within a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_n_t_a_c_t__m_a_t_r_i_x.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">GROUP<div class="right">specifies the list of atoms that should be assumed indistinguishable<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datq61s">q61s</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">specify the switching function to use between two sets of indistinguishable atoms<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0}

<b name="data/driver.datlq61s_prod" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datlq61s_prod","data/driver.datlq61s_prod","red")'>lq61s_prod</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datlq61s_prod">The CUSTOM action with label <b>lq61s_prod</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">lq61s_prod</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>the matrix obtained by doing an element-wise application of an arbitrary function to the input matrix</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CUSTOM<div class="right">Calculate a combination of variables using a custom expression. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_u_s_t_o_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datlq61s_cmap">lq61s_cmap</b>,<b name="data/driver.datlq61s_dpmat">lq61s_dpmat</b> <div class="tooltip">FUNC<div class="right">the function you wish to evaluate<i></i></div></div>=x*y <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<b name="data/driver.datlq61s_ones" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datlq61s_ones","data/driver.datlq61s_ones","blue")'>lq61s_ones</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datlq61s_ones">The CONSTANT action with label <b>lq61s_ones</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">lq61s_ones</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the constant value that was read from the plumed input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ONES<div class="right">Create a constant vector with all elements equal to one <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_o_n_e_s.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SIZE<div class="right">the number of ones that you would like to create<i></i></div></div>=370

<span style="color:blue"># --- End of included input --- </span></span><br/><span id="data/driver.datcn4_short"><b name="data/driver.datcn4" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn4","data/driver.datcn4_shortcut","blue")'>cn4</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn4_shortcut">The COORDINATIONNUMBER action with label <b>cn4</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn4</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the value calculated by this action</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">COORDINATIONNUMBER<div class="right">Calculate the coordination numbers of atoms so that you can then calculate functions of the distribution of This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datcn4");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_o_r_d_i_n_a_t_i_o_n_n_u_m_b_e_r.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESA<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datflq60s">flq60s</b> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESB<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datflq61s">flq61s</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">the switching function that it used in the construction of the contact matrix<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0}

</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datcn4")'># cn4: COORDINATIONNUMBER SPECIESA=flq60s SPECIESB=flq61s SWITCH={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} </span>

<b name="data/driver.datcn4_mat" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn4_mat","data/driver.datcn4_mat","red")'>cn4_mat</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn4_mat">The CONTACT_MATRIX action with label <b>cn4_mat</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn4_mat</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>a matrix containing the weights for the bonds between each pair of atoms</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CONTACT_MATRIX<div class="right">Adjacency matrix in which two atoms are adjacent if they are within a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_n_t_a_c_t__m_a_t_r_i_x.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">specify the switching function to use between two sets of indistinguishable atoms<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0}

<b name="data/driver.datcn4" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn4","data/driver.datcn4","blue")'>cn4</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn4">The MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT action with label <b>cn4</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn4</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector that is obtained by taking the product between the matrix and the vector that were input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT<div class="right">Calculate the product of the matrix and the vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_a_t_r_i_x__v_e_c_t_o_r__p_r_o_d_u_c_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn4_mat">cn4_mat</b>,<b name="data/driver.datcn4_ones">cn4_ones</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcn4_caverage" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn4_caverage","data/driver.datcn4_caverage","black")'>cn4_caverage</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn4_caverage">The MEAN action with label <b>cn4_caverage</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn4_caverage</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the mean of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MEAN<div class="right">Calculate the arithmetic mean of the elements in a vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_e_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn4">cn4</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<span style="color:blue"># --- End of included input --- </span></span><span id="data/driver.datcn5_short"><b name="data/driver.datcn5" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn5","data/driver.datcn5_shortcut","blue")'>cn5</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn5_shortcut">The COORDINATIONNUMBER action with label <b>cn5</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn5</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the value calculated by this action</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">COORDINATIONNUMBER<div class="right">Calculate the coordination numbers of atoms so that you can then calculate functions of the distribution of This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datcn5");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_o_r_d_i_n_a_t_i_o_n_n_u_m_b_e_r.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESA<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datflq61s">flq61s</b> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESB<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datflq60s">flq60s</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">the switching function that it used in the construction of the contact matrix<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} 
</span><span id="data/driver.datcn5_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datcn5")'># cn5: COORDINATIONNUMBER SPECIESA=flq61s SPECIESB=flq60s SWITCH={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} </span>
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># as follows (Click the red comment above to revert to the short version of the input):</span>
<b name="data/driver.datcn5_grp" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn5_grp","data/driver.datcn5_grp","violet")'>cn5_grp</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn5_grp">The GROUP action with label <b>cn5_grp</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn5_grp</td><td width="5%"><font color="violet">atoms</font></td><td>indices of atoms specified in GROUP</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">GROUP<div class="right">Define a group of atoms so that a particular list of atoms can be referenced with a single label in definitions of CVs or virtual atoms. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_g_r_o_u_p.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOMS<div class="right">the numerical indexes for the set of atoms in the group<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datflq61s">flq61s</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcn5_mat" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn5_mat","data/driver.datcn5_mat","red")'>cn5_mat</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn5_mat">The CONTACT_MATRIX action with label <b>cn5_mat</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn5_mat</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>a matrix containing the weights for the bonds between each pair of atoms</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CONTACT_MATRIX<div class="right">Adjacency matrix in which two atoms are adjacent if they are within a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_n_t_a_c_t__m_a_t_r_i_x.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">specify the switching function to use between two sets of indistinguishable atoms<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} 
<b name="data/driver.datcn5_ones" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn5_ones","data/driver.datcn5_ones","blue")'>cn5_ones</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn5_ones">The CONSTANT action with label <b>cn5_ones</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn5_ones</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the constant value that was read from the plumed input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ONES<div class="right">Create a constant vector with all elements equal to one <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_o_n_e_s.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SIZE<div class="right">the number of ones that you would like to create<i></i></div></div>=370
<b name="data/driver.datcn5" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn5","data/driver.datcn5","blue")'>cn5</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn5">The MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT action with label <b>cn5</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn5</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector that is obtained by taking the product between the matrix and the vector that were input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT<div class="right">Calculate the product of the matrix and the vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_a_t_r_i_x__v_e_c_t_o_r__p_r_o_d_u_c_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn5_mat">cn5_mat</b>,<b name="data/driver.datcn5_ones">cn5_ones</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcn5_caverage" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn5_caverage","data/driver.datcn5_caverage","black")'>cn5_caverage</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn5_caverage">The MEAN action with label <b>cn5_caverage</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn5_caverage</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the mean of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MEAN<div class="right">Calculate the arithmetic mean of the elements in a vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_e_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn5">cn5</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<span style="color:blue"># --- End of included input --- </span></span><br/><span id="data/driver.datsphere2_short"><b name="data/driver.datsphere2" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datsphere2","data/driver.datsphere2_shortcut","blue")'>sphere2</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datsphere2_shortcut">The INSPHERE action with label <b>sphere2</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">sphere2_morethan</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the number of cvs in the region of interest that are more than a certain threshold</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">INSPHERE<div class="right">This quantity can be used to calculate functions of the distribution of collective variables for the atoms that lie in a particular, user-specified part of of the cell. This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datsphere2");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_i_n_s_p_h_e_r_e.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOM<div class="right">the atom whose vicinity we are interested in examining<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datp">p</b> <div class="tooltip">DATA<div class="right">the label of an action that calculates multicolvars<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn4">cn4</b> <div class="tooltip">RADIUS<div class="right">the switching function that tells us the extent of the sphereical region of interest<i></i></div></div>={TANH R_0=1.5}  <div class="tooltip">MORE_THAN<div class="right">calcualte the number of colvars that are inside the region of interest and that are greater that a certain threshold<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=5.0}
</span><span id="data/driver.datsphere2_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datsphere2")'># sphere2: INSPHERE ATOM=p DATA=cn4 RADIUS={TANH R_0=1.5}  MORE_THAN={RATIONAL R_0=5.0}</span>
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># as follows (Click the red comment above to revert to the short version of the input):</span>
<b name="data/driver.datsphere2_grp" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datsphere2_grp","data/driver.datsphere2_grp","violet")'>sphere2_grp</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datsphere2_grp">The GROUP action with label <b>sphere2_grp</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">sphere2_grp</td><td width="5%"><font color="violet">atoms</font></td><td>indices of atoms specified in GROUP</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">GROUP<div class="right">Define a group of atoms so that a particular list of atoms can be referenced with a single label in definitions of CVs or virtual atoms. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_g_r_o_u_p.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOMS<div class="right">the numerical indexes for the set of atoms in the group<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn4">cn4</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcn4_sphere2_mt" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn4_sphere2_mt","data/driver.datcn4_sphere2_mt","blue")'>cn4_sphere2_mt</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn4_sphere2_mt">The MORE_THAN action with label <b>cn4_sphere2_mt</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn4_sphere2_mt</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of a function that is one if the if the input is more than a threshold to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MORE_THAN<div class="right">Use a switching function to determine how many of the input variables are more than a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_o_r_e__t_h_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn4">cn4</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">This keyword is used if you want to employ an alternative to the continuous swiching function defined above<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=5.0} 
<b name="data/driver.datsphere2_mt" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datsphere2_mt","data/driver.datsphere2_mt","blue")'>sphere2_mt</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datsphere2_mt">The CUSTOM action with label <b>sphere2_mt</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">sphere2_mt</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of an arbitrary function to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CUSTOM<div class="right">Calculate a combination of variables using a custom expression. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_u_s_t_o_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn4_sphere2_mt">cn4_sphere2_mt</b>,sphere2 <div class="tooltip">FUNC<div class="right">the function you wish to evaluate<i></i></div></div>=x*y <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<b name="data/driver.datsphere2_morethan" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datsphere2_morethan","data/driver.datsphere2_morethan","black")'>sphere2_morethan</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datsphere2_morethan">The SUM action with label <b>sphere2_morethan</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">sphere2_morethan</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the sum of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">SUM<div class="right">Calculate the sum of the arguments <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_s_u_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datsphere2_mt">sphere2_mt</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO

<b name="data/driver.datcn5_sphere3_mt" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn5_sphere3_mt","data/driver.datcn5_sphere3_mt","blue")'>cn5_sphere3_mt</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn5_sphere3_mt">The MORE_THAN action with label <b>cn5_sphere3_mt</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn5_sphere3_mt</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of a function that is one if the if the input is more than a threshold to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MORE_THAN<div class="right">Use a switching function to determine how many of the input variables are more than a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_o_r_e__t_h_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn5">cn5</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">This keyword is used if you want to employ an alternative to the continuous swiching function defined above<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=5.0} 
<b name="data/driver.datsphere3_mt" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datsphere3_mt","data/driver.datsphere3_mt","blue")'>sphere3_mt</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datsphere3_mt">The CUSTOM action with label <b>sphere3_mt</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">sphere3_mt</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of an arbitrary function to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CUSTOM<div class="right">Calculate a combination of variables using a custom expression. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_u_s_t_o_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn5_sphere3_mt">cn5_sphere3_mt</b>,sphere3 <div class="tooltip">FUNC<div class="right">the function you wish to evaluate<i></i></div></div>=x*y <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<b name="data/driver.datsphere3_morethan" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datsphere3_morethan","data/driver.datsphere3_morethan","black")'>sphere3_morethan</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datsphere3_morethan">The SUM action with label <b>sphere3_morethan</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">sphere3_morethan</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the sum of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">SUM<div class="right">Calculate the sum of the arguments <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_s_u_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datsphere3_mt">sphere3_mt</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<span style="color:blue"># --- End of included input --- </span></span><br/><b name="data/driver.datqncrysts" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datqncrysts","data/driver.datqncrysts","black")'>qncrysts</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datqncrysts">The COMBINE action with label <b>qncrysts</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">qncrysts</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>a linear compbination</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">COMBINE<div class="right">Calculate a polynomial combination of a set of other variables. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_m_b_i_n_e.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=sphere2.morethan,sphere3.morethan <div class="tooltip">POWERS<div class="right"> the powers to which you are raising each of the arguments in your function<i></i></div></div>=1,1 <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO
<b name="data/driver.datqvcrysts" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datqvcrysts","data/driver.datqvcrysts","black")'>qvcrysts</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datqvcrysts">The COMBINE action with label <b>qvcrysts</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">qvcrysts</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>a linear compbination</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">COMBINE<div class="right">Calculate a polynomial combination of a set of other variables. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_m_b_i_n_e.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datqncrysts">qncrysts</b> <div class="tooltip">POWERS<div class="right"> the powers to which you are raising each of the arguments in your function<i></i></div></div>=0.333 <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO

<br/><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># Measure coordination of crystalline ions in the box</span>
<span id="data/driver.datcn6_short"><b name="data/driver.datcn6" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn6","data/driver.datcn6_shortcut","blue")'>cn6</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn6_shortcut">The COORDINATIONNUMBER action with label <b>cn6</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn6</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the value calculated by this action</td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn6_morethan</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the number of colvars that have a value more than a threshold</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">COORDINATIONNUMBER<div class="right">Calculate the coordination numbers of atoms so that you can then calculate functions of the distribution of This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datcn6");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_o_r_d_i_n_a_t_i_o_n_n_u_m_b_e_r.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESA<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datflq60s">flq60s</b> <div class="tooltip">SPECIESB<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as the coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datflq61s">flq61s</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">the switching function that it used in the construction of the contact matrix<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} <div class="tooltip">MORE_THAN<div class="right">calculate the number of variables that are more than a certain target value<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=5.0}
</span><span id="data/driver.datcn6_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datcn6")'># cn6: COORDINATIONNUMBER SPECIESA=flq60s SPECIESB=flq61s SWITCH={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} MORE_THAN={RATIONAL R_0=5.0}</span>
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># as follows (Click the red comment above to revert to the short version of the input):</span>
<b name="data/driver.datcn6_grp" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn6_grp","data/driver.datcn6_grp","violet")'>cn6_grp</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn6_grp">The GROUP action with label <b>cn6_grp</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn6_grp</td><td width="5%"><font color="violet">atoms</font></td><td>indices of atoms specified in GROUP</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">GROUP<div class="right">Define a group of atoms so that a particular list of atoms can be referenced with a single label in definitions of CVs or virtual atoms. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_g_r_o_u_p.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOMS<div class="right">the numerical indexes for the set of atoms in the group<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datflq60s">flq60s</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcn6_mat" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn6_mat","data/driver.datcn6_mat","red")'>cn6_mat</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn6_mat">The CONTACT_MATRIX action with label <b>cn6_mat</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn6_mat</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>a matrix containing the weights for the bonds between each pair of atoms</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CONTACT_MATRIX<div class="right">Adjacency matrix in which two atoms are adjacent if they are within a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_n_t_a_c_t__m_a_t_r_i_x.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">specify the switching function to use between two sets of indistinguishable atoms<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.38 D_MAX=1.0} 
<b name="data/driver.datcn6_ones" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn6_ones","data/driver.datcn6_ones","blue")'>cn6_ones</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn6_ones">The CONSTANT action with label <b>cn6_ones</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn6_ones</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the constant value that was read from the plumed input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ONES<div class="right">Create a constant vector with all elements equal to one <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_o_n_e_s.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SIZE<div class="right">the number of ones that you would like to create<i></i></div></div>=370
<b name="data/driver.datcn6" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn6","data/driver.datcn6","blue")'>cn6</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn6">The MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT action with label <b>cn6</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn6</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector that is obtained by taking the product between the matrix and the vector that were input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT<div class="right">Calculate the product of the matrix and the vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_a_t_r_i_x__v_e_c_t_o_r__p_r_o_d_u_c_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn6_mat">cn6_mat</b>,<b name="data/driver.datcn6_ones">cn6_ones</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcn6_caverage" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn6_caverage","data/driver.datcn6_caverage","black")'>cn6_caverage</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn6_caverage">The MEAN action with label <b>cn6_caverage</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn6_caverage</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the mean of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MEAN<div class="right">Calculate the arithmetic mean of the elements in a vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_e_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn6">cn6</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<b name="data/driver.datcn6_mt" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn6_mt","data/driver.datcn6_mt","blue")'>cn6_mt</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn6_mt">The MORE_THAN action with label <b>cn6_mt</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn6_mt</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of a function that is one if the if the input is more than a threshold to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MORE_THAN<div class="right">Use a switching function to determine how many of the input variables are more than a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_o_r_e__t_h_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn6">cn6</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">This keyword is used if you want to employ an alternative to the continuous swiching function defined above<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=5.0}

<b name="data/driver.datcn7_ones" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn7_ones","data/driver.datcn7_ones","blue")'>cn7_ones</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn7_ones">The CONSTANT action with label <b>cn7_ones</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn7_ones</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the constant value that was read from the plumed input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ONES<div class="right">Create a constant vector with all elements equal to one <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_o_n_e_s.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SIZE<div class="right">the number of ones that you would like to create<i></i></div></div>=370
<b name="data/driver.datcn7" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn7","data/driver.datcn7","blue")'>cn7</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn7">The MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT action with label <b>cn7</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn7</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector that is obtained by taking the product between the matrix and the vector that were input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT<div class="right">Calculate the product of the matrix and the vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_a_t_r_i_x__v_e_c_t_o_r__p_r_o_d_u_c_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn7_mat">cn7_mat</b>,<b name="data/driver.datcn7_ones">cn7_ones</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcn7_caverage" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn7_caverage","data/driver.datcn7_caverage","black")'>cn7_caverage</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn7_caverage">The MEAN action with label <b>cn7_caverage</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn7_caverage</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the mean of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MEAN<div class="right">Calculate the arithmetic mean of the elements in a vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_e_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn7">cn7</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<b name="data/driver.datcn7_mt" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn7_mt","data/driver.datcn7_mt","blue")'>cn7_mt</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn7_mt">The MORE_THAN action with label <b>cn7_mt</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn7_mt</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of a function that is one if the if the input is more than a threshold to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MORE_THAN<div class="right">Use a switching function to determine how many of the input variables are more than a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_o_r_e__t_h_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn7">cn7</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">This keyword is used if you want to employ an alternative to the continuous swiching function defined above<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=5.0} 
<b name="data/driver.datcn7_morethan" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcn7_morethan","data/driver.datcn7_morethan","black")'>cn7_morethan</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcn7_morethan">The SUM action with label <b>cn7_morethan</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cn7_morethan</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the sum of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">SUM<div class="right">Calculate the sum of the arguments <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_s_u_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn7_mt">cn7_mt</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<span style="color:blue"># --- End of included input --- </span></span><br/><b name="data/driver.datqncrysta" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datqncrysta","data/driver.datqncrysta","black")'>qncrysta</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datqncrysta">The COMBINE action with label <b>qncrysta</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">qncrysta</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>a linear compbination</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">COMBINE<div class="right">Calculate a polynomial combination of a set of other variables. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_m_b_i_n_e.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcn6">cn6.morethan</b>,<b name="data/driver.datcn7">cn7.morethan</b> <div class="tooltip">POWERS<div class="right"> the powers to which you are raising each of the arguments in your function<i></i></div></div>=1,1 <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO
<b name="data/driver.datqvcrysta" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datqvcrysta","data/driver.datqvcrysta","black")'>qvcrysta</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datqvcrysta">The COMBINE action with label <b>qvcrysta</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">qvcrysta</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>a linear compbination</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">COMBINE<div class="right">Calculate a polynomial combination of a set of other variables. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_m_b_i_n_e.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datqncrysta">qncrysta</b> <div class="tooltip">POWERS<div class="right"> the powers to which you are raising each of the arguments in your function<i></i></div></div>=0.333 <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO

<br/><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># Print</span>
<div class="tooltip" style="color:green">PRINT<div class="right">Print quantities to a file. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_p_r_i_n_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datncrysts">ncrysts</b>,<b name="data/driver.datvcrysts">vcrysts</b>,<b name="data/driver.datqncrysts">qncrysts</b>,<b name="data/driver.datqvcrysts">qvcrysts</b>,<b name="data/driver.datncrysta">ncrysta</b>,<b name="data/driver.datvcrysta">vcrysta</b>,<b name="data/driver.datqncrysta">qncrysta</b>,<b name="data/driver.datqvcrysta">qvcrysta</b> <div class="tooltip">FILE<div class="right">the name of the file on which to output these quantities<i></i></div></div>=q6-sphere-and-all.dat
<br/><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># histograms for the rxn coordinate variables</span>
<span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.dat">The PRINT action with label <b></b> calculates something</span><span id="data/driver.dathistcv1_short"><span id="data/driver.datdefhistcv1_short"><b name="data/driver.dathistcv1" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.dathistcv1","data/driver.dathistcv1_shortcut","green")'>histcv1</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.dathistcv1_shortcut">The HISTOGRAM action with label <b>histcv1</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">histcv1</td><td width="5%"><font color="green">grid</font></td><td>the estimate of the histogram as a function of the argument that was obtained</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">HISTOGRAM<div class="right">Accumulate the average probability density along a few CVs from a trajectory. This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.dathistcv1");'>a shortcut</a> and it has <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datdefhistcv1");'>hidden defaults</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_h_i_s_t_o_g_r_a_m.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the quantity that is being averaged<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datvcrysta">vcrysta</b> <div class="tooltip">GRID_MIN<div class="right"> the lower bounds for the grid<i></i></div></div>=-1 <div class="tooltip">GRID_MAX<div class="right"> the upper bounds for the grid<i></i></div></div>=10 <div class="tooltip">GRID_BIN<div class="right">the number of bins for the grid<i></i></div></div>=200 <div class="tooltip">BANDWIDTH<div class="right">the bandwidths for kernel density esimtation<i></i></div></div>=0.01
</span><span id="data/driver.datdefhistcv1_long" style="display:none;"><b name="data/driver.dathistcv1" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.dathistcv1","data/driver.dathistcv1_shortcut","green")'>histcv1</b>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">HISTOGRAM<div class="right">Accumulate the average probability density along a few CVs from a trajectory. This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.dathistcv1");'>a shortcut</a> and uses the <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datdefhistcv1");'>defaults shown here</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_h_i_s_t_o_g_r_a_m.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the quantity that is being averaged<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datvcrysta">vcrysta</b> <div class="tooltip">GRID_MIN<div class="right"> the lower bounds for the grid<i></i></div></div>=-1 <div class="tooltip">GRID_MAX<div class="right"> the upper bounds for the grid<i></i></div></div>=10 <div class="tooltip">GRID_BIN<div class="right">the number of bins for the grid<i></i></div></div>=200 <div class="tooltip">BANDWIDTH<div class="right">the bandwidths for kernel density esimtation<i></i></div></div>=0.01  <div class="tooltip">NORMALIZATION<div class="right"> This controls how the data is normalized it can be set equal to true, false or ndata<i></i></div></div>=ndata <div class="tooltip">STRIDE<div class="right"> the frequency with which to store data for averaging<i></i></div></div>=1 <div class="tooltip">CLEAR<div class="right"> the frequency with whihc to clear the data that is being averaged<i></i></div></div>=0
</span></span><span id="data/driver.dathistcv1_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.dathistcv1")'># histcv1: HISTOGRAM ARG=vcrysta GRID_MIN=-1. GRID_MAX=10. GRID_BIN=200 BANDWIDTH=0.01</span>
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># as follows (Click the red comment above to revert to the short version of the input):</span>
<b name="data/driver.dathistcv1_weight" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.dathistcv1_weight","data/driver.dathistcv1_weight","black")'>histcv1_weight</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.dathistcv1_weight">The CONSTANT action with label <b>histcv1_weight</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">histcv1_weight</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the constant value that was read from the plumed input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ONES<div class="right">Create a constant vector with all elements equal to one <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_o_n_e_s.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SIZE<div class="right">the number of ones that you would like to create<i></i></div></div>=1
<b name="data/driver.dathistcv1_kde" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.dathistcv1_kde","data/driver.dathistcv1_kde","green")'>histcv1_kde</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.dathistcv1_kde">The KDE action with label <b>histcv1_kde</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">histcv1_kde</td><td width="5%"><font color="green">grid</font></td><td>a function on a grid that was obtained by doing a Kernel Density Estimation using the input arguments</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">KDE<div class="right">Create a histogram from the input scalar/vector/matrix using KDE <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_k_d_e.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datvcrysta">vcrysta</b> <div class="tooltip">GRID_MIN<div class="right"> the lower bounds for the grid<i></i></div></div>=-1 <div class="tooltip">GRID_MAX<div class="right"> the upper bounds for the grid<i></i></div></div>=10 <div class="tooltip">GRID_BIN<div class="right">the number of bins for the grid<i></i></div></div>=200 <div class="tooltip">BANDWIDTH<div class="right">the bandwidths for kernel density esimtation<i></i></div></div>=0.01 
<b name="data/driver.dathistcv1_kdep" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.dathistcv1_kdep","data/driver.dathistcv1_kdep","green")'>histcv1_kdep</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.dathistcv1_kdep">The CUSTOM action with label <b>histcv1_kdep</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">histcv1_kdep</td><td width="5%"><font color="green">grid</font></td><td>the grid obtained by doing an element-wise application of an arbitrary function to the input grid</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CUSTOM<div class="right">Calculate a combination of variables using a custom expression. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_u_s_t_o_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.dathistcv1_kde">histcv1_kde</b>,<b name="data/driver.dathistcv1_weight">histcv1_weight</b> <div class="tooltip">FUNC<div class="right">the function you wish to evaluate<i></i></div></div>=x*y <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<b name="data/driver.dathistcv1_u" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.dathistcv1_u","data/driver.dathistcv1_u","green")'>histcv1_u</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.dathistcv1_u">The ACCUMULATE action with label <b>histcv1_u</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">histcv1_u</td><td width="5%"><font color="green">grid</font></td><td>a sum calculated from the time series of the input quantity</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ACCUMULATE<div class="right">Sum the elements of this value over the course of the trajectory <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_a_c_c_u_m_u_l_a_t_e.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.dathistcv1_kdep">histcv1_kdep</b> <div class="tooltip">STRIDE<div class="right"> the frequency with which the data should be collected and added to the quantity being averaged<i></i></div></div>=1 <div class="tooltip">CLEAR<div class="right"> the frequency with which to clear all the accumulated data<i></i></div></div>=0 
<b name="data/driver.dathistcv1_nsum" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.dathistcv1_nsum","data/driver.dathistcv1_nsum","black")'>histcv1_nsum</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.dathistcv1_nsum">The ACCUMULATE action with label <b>histcv1_nsum</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">histcv1_nsum</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>a sum calculated from the time series of the input quantity</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ACCUMULATE<div class="right">Sum the elements of this value over the course of the trajectory <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_a_c_c_u_m_u_l_a_t_e.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.dathistcv1_weight">histcv1_weight</b> <div class="tooltip">STRIDE<div class="right"> the frequency with which the data should be collected and added to the quantity being averaged<i></i></div></div>=1 <div class="tooltip">CLEAR<div class="right"> the frequency with which to clear all the accumulated data<i></i></div></div>=0 
<b name="data/driver.dathistcv1" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.dathistcv1","data/driver.dathistcv1","green")'>histcv1</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.dathistcv1">The CUSTOM action with label <b>histcv1</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">histcv1</td><td width="5%"><font color="green">grid</font></td><td>the grid obtained by doing an element-wise application of an arbitrary function to the input grid</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CUSTOM<div class="right">Calculate a combination of variables using a custom expression. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_u_s_t_o_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.dathistcv1_u">histcv1_u</b>,<b name="data/driver.dathistcv1_nsum">histcv1_nsum</b> <div class="tooltip">FUNC<div class="right">the function you wish to evaluate<i></i></div></div>=x/y <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO
