This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.dathistcv2");'>a shortcut</a> and it has <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datdefhistcv2");'>hidden defaults</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_h_i_s_t_o_g_r_a_m.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the quantity that is being averaged<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datqvcrysta">qvcrysta</b> <div class="tooltip">GRID_MIN<div class="right"> the lower bounds for the grid<i></i></div></div>=-1 <div class="tooltip">GRID_MAX<div class="right"> the upper bounds for the grid<i></i></div></div>=10 <div class="tooltip">GRID_BIN<div class="right">the number of bins for the grid<i></i></div></div>=200 <div class="tooltip">BANDWIDTH<div class="right">the bandwidths for kernel density esimtation<i></i></div></div>=0.01
</span><span id="data/driver.datdefhistcv2_long" style="display:none;"><b name="data/driver.dathistcv2" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.dathistcv2","data/driver.dathistcv2_shortcut","green")'>histcv2</b>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">HISTOGRAM<div class="right">Accumulate the average probability density along a few CVs from a trajectory. This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.dathistcv2");'>a shortcut</a> and uses the <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datdefhistcv2");'>defaults shown here</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_h_i_s_t_o_g_r_a_m.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the quantity that is being averaged<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datqvcrysta">qvcrysta</b> <div class="tooltip">GRID_MIN<div class="right"> the lower bounds for the grid<i></i></div></div>=-1 <div class="tooltip">GRID_MAX<div class="right"> the upper bounds for the grid<i></i></div></div>=10 <div class="tooltip">GRID_BIN<div class="right">the number of bins for the grid<i></i></div></div>=200 <div class="tooltip">BANDWIDTH<div class="right">the bandwidths for kernel density esimtation<i></i></div></div>=0.01  <div class="tooltip">NORMALIZATION<div class="right"> This controls how the data is normalized it can be set equal to true, false or ndata<i></i></div></div>=ndata <div class="tooltip">STRIDE<div class="right"> the frequency with which to store data for averaging<i></i></div></div>=1 <div class="tooltip">CLEAR<div class="right"> the frequency with whihc to clear the data that is being averaged<i></i></div></div>=0
</span></span><span id="data/driver.dathistcv2_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.dathistcv2")'># histcv2: HISTOGRAM ARG=qvcrysta GRID_MIN=-1. GRID_MAX=10. GRID_BIN=200 BANDWIDTH=0.01</span>
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># as follows (Click the red comment above to revert to the short version of the input):</span>
<b name="data/driver.dathistcv2_weight" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.dathistcv2_weight","data/driver.dathistcv2_weight","black")'>histcv2_weight</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.dathistcv2_weight">The CONSTANT action with label <b>histcv2_weight</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">histcv2_weight</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the constant value that was read from the plumed input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ONES<div class="right">Create a constant vector with all elements equal to one <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_o_n_e_s.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SIZE<div class="right">the number of ones that you would like to create<i></i></div></div>=1
<b name="data/driver.dathistcv2_kde" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.dathistcv2_kde","data/driver.dathistcv2_kde","green")'>histcv2_kde</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.dathistcv2_kde">The KDE action with label <b>histcv2_kde</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">histcv2_kde</td><td width="5%"><font color="green">grid</font></td><td>a function on a grid that was obtained by doing a Kernel Density Estimation using the input arguments</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">KDE<div class="right">Create a histogram from the input scalar/vector/matrix using KDE <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_k_d_e.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datqvcrysta">qvcrysta</b> <div class="tooltip">GRID_MIN<div class="right"> the lower bounds for the grid<i></i></div></div>=-1 <div class="tooltip">GRID_MAX<div class="right"> the upper bounds for the grid<i></i></div></div>=10 <div class="tooltip">GRID_BIN<div class="right">the number of bins for the grid<i></i></div></div>=200 <div class="tooltip">BANDWIDTH<div class="right">the bandwidths for kernel density esimtation<i></i></div></div>=0.01 
<b name="data/driver.dathistcv2_kdep" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.dathistcv2_kdep","data/driver.dathistcv2_kdep","green")'>histcv2_kdep</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.dathistcv2_kdep">The CUSTOM action with label <b>histcv2_kdep</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">histcv2_kdep</td><td width="5%"><font color="green">grid</font></td><td>the grid obtained by doing an element-wise application of an arbitrary function to the input grid</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CUSTOM<div class="right">Calculate a combination of variables using a custom expression. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_u_s_t_o_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.dathistcv2_kde">histcv2_kde</b>,<b name="data/driver.dathistcv2_weight">histcv2_weight</b> <div class="tooltip">FUNC<div class="right">the function you wish to evaluate<i></i></div></div>=x*y <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<b name="data/driver.dathistcv2_u" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.dathistcv2_u","data/driver.dathistcv2_u","green")'>histcv2_u</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.dathistcv2_u">The ACCUMULATE action with label <b>histcv2_u</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">histcv2_u</td><td width="5%"><font color="green">grid</font></td><td>a sum calculated from the time series of the input quantity</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ACCUMULATE<div class="right">Sum the elements of this value over the course of the trajectory <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_a_c_c_u_m_u_l_a_t_e.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.dathistcv2_kdep">histcv2_kdep</b> <div class="tooltip">STRIDE<div class="right"> the frequency with which the data should be collected and added to the quantity being averaged<i></i></div></div>=1 <div class="tooltip">CLEAR<div class="right"> the frequency with which to clear all the accumulated data<i></i></div></div>=0 
<b name="data/driver.dathistcv2_nsum" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.dathistcv2_nsum","data/driver.dathistcv2_nsum","black")'>histcv2_nsum</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.dathistcv2_nsum">The ACCUMULATE action with label <b>histcv2_nsum</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">histcv2_nsum</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>a sum calculated from the time series of the input quantity</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ACCUMULATE<div class="right">Sum the elements of this value over the course of the trajectory <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_a_c_c_u_m_u_l_a_t_e.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.dathistcv2_weight">histcv2_weight</b> <div class="tooltip">STRIDE<div class="right"> the frequency with which the data should be collected and added to the quantity being averaged<i></i></div></div>=1 <div class="tooltip">CLEAR<div class="right"> the frequency with which to clear all the accumulated data<i></i></div></div>=0 
<b name="data/driver.dathistcv2" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.dathistcv2","data/driver.dathistcv2","green")'>histcv2</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.dathistcv2">The CUSTOM action with label <b>histcv2</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">histcv2</td><td width="5%"><font color="green">grid</font></td><td>the grid obtained by doing an element-wise application of an arbitrary function to the input grid</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CUSTOM<div class="right">Calculate a combination of variables using a custom expression. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_u_s_t_o_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.dathistcv2_u">histcv2_u</b>,<b name="data/driver.dathistcv2_nsum">histcv2_nsum</b> <div class="tooltip">FUNC<div class="right">the function you wish to evaluate<i></i></div></div>=x/y <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<span style="color:blue"># --- End of included input --- </span></span><div class="tooltip" style="color:green">DUMPGRID<div class="right">Output the function on the grid to a file with the PLUMED grid format. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_d_u_m_p_g_r_i_d.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">GRID<div class="right">the grid you would like to print (can also use ARG for specifying what is being printed)<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.dathistcv1">histcv1</b> <div class="tooltip">FILE<div class="right"> the file on which to write the grid<i></i></div></div>=histn.dat <div class="tooltip">FMT<div class="right">the format that should be used to output real numbers<i></i></div></div>=%8.4f
<div class="tooltip" style="color:green">DUMPGRID<div class="right">Output the function on the grid to a file with the PLUMED grid format. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_d_u_m_p_g_r_i_d.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">GRID<div class="right">the grid you would like to print (can also use ARG for specifying what is being printed)<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.dathistcv2">histcv2</b> <div class="tooltip">FILE<div class="right"> the file on which to write the grid<i></i></div></div>=histnq6.dat <div class="tooltip">FMT<div class="right">the format that should be used to output real numbers<i></i></div></div>=%8.4f


<br/><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># Get the Na-Cl coordination number </span>
<span id="data/driver.datcnint_short"><b name="data/driver.datcnint" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcnint","data/driver.datcnint_shortcut","blue")'>cnint</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcnint_shortcut">The COORDINATIONNUMBER action with label <b>cnint</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cnint</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the value calculated by this action</td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cnint_highest</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the largest of the colvars</td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cnint_mean</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the mean of the colvars</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">COORDINATIONNUMBER<div class="right">Calculate the coordination numbers of atoms so that you can then calculate functions of the distribution of This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datcnint");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_o_r_d_i_n_a_t_i_o_n_n_u_m_b_e_r.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SPECIES<div class="right">this keyword is used for colvars such as coordination number<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgions">gions</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">the switching function that it used in the construction of the contact matrix<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.355 D_MAX=0.36} <div class="tooltip">MEAN<div class="right"> calculate the mean of all the quantities<i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">HIGHEST<div class="right"> this flag allows you to recover the highest of these variables<i></i></div></div>
</span><span id="data/driver.datcnint_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datcnint")'># cnint: COORDINATIONNUMBER SPECIES=gions SWITCH={RATIONAL R_0=0.355 D_MAX=0.36} MEAN HIGHEST</span>
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># as follows (Click the red comment above to revert to the short version of the input):</span>
<b name="data/driver.datcnint_grp" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcnint_grp","data/driver.datcnint_grp","violet")'>cnint_grp</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcnint_grp">The GROUP action with label <b>cnint_grp</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cnint_grp</td><td width="5%"><font color="violet">atoms</font></td><td>indices of atoms specified in GROUP</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">GROUP<div class="right">Define a group of atoms so that a particular list of atoms can be referenced with a single label in definitions of CVs or virtual atoms. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_g_r_o_u_p.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOMS<div class="right">the numerical indexes for the set of atoms in the group<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgions">gions</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcnint_mat" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcnint_mat","data/driver.datcnint_mat","red")'>cnint_mat</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcnint_mat">The CONTACT_MATRIX action with label <b>cnint_mat</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cnint_mat</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>a matrix containing the weights for the bonds between each pair of atoms</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CONTACT_MATRIX<div class="right">Adjacency matrix in which two atoms are adjacent if they are within a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_n_t_a_c_t__m_a_t_r_i_x.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">GROUP<div class="right">specifies the list of atoms that should be assumed indistinguishable<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datgions">gions</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">specify the switching function to use between two sets of indistinguishable atoms<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.355 D_MAX=0.36} 
<b name="data/driver.datcnint_ones" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcnint_ones","data/driver.datcnint_ones","blue")'>cnint_ones</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcnint_ones">The CONSTANT action with label <b>cnint_ones</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cnint_ones</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the constant value that was read from the plumed input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">ONES<div class="right">Create a constant vector with all elements equal to one <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_o_n_e_s.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">SIZE<div class="right">the number of ones that you would like to create<i></i></div></div>=740
<b name="data/driver.datcnint" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcnint","data/driver.datcnint","blue")'>cnint</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcnint">The MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT action with label <b>cnint</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cnint</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector that is obtained by taking the product between the matrix and the vector that were input</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MATRIX_VECTOR_PRODUCT<div class="right">Calculate the product of the matrix and the vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_a_t_r_i_x__v_e_c_t_o_r__p_r_o_d_u_c_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcnint_mat">cnint_mat</b>,<b name="data/driver.datcnint_ones">cnint_ones</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcnint_caverage" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcnint_caverage","data/driver.datcnint_caverage","black")'>cnint_caverage</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcnint_caverage">The MEAN action with label <b>cnint_caverage</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cnint_caverage</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the mean of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MEAN<div class="right">Calculate the arithmetic mean of the elements in a vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_e_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcnint">cnint</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<b name="data/driver.datcnint_highest" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcnint_highest","data/driver.datcnint_highest","black")'>cnint_highest</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcnint_highest">The HIGHEST action with label <b>cnint_highest</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cnint_highest</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the largest element of the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">HIGHEST<div class="right">This function can be used to find the highest colvar by magnitude in a set. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_h_i_g_h_e_s_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcnint">cnint</b> 
<b name="data/driver.datcnint_mean" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datcnint_mean","data/driver.datcnint_mean","black")'>cnint_mean</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datcnint_mean">The MEAN action with label <b>cnint_mean</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">cnint_mean</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the mean of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MEAN<div class="right">Calculate the arithmetic mean of the elements in a vector <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_e_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcnint">cnint</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO 
<span style="color:blue"># --- End of included input --- </span></span><br/><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># Report the mean and highest coordination numbers </span>
<div class="tooltip" style="color:green">PRINT<div class="right">Print quantities to a file. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_p_r_i_n_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcnint">cnint</b> <div class="tooltip">FILE<div class="right">the name of the file on which to output these quantities<i></i></div></div>=cn-regions.dat
<br/><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># DFS on ion clusters built according to simple cut-off criterion</span>
<b name="data/driver.datmat" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datmat","data/driver.datmat","red")'>mat</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datmat">The CONTACT_MATRIX action with label <b>mat</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">mat</td><td width="5%"><font color="red">matrix</font></td><td>a matrix containing the weights for the bonds between each pair of atoms</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CONTACT_MATRIX<div class="right">Adjacency matrix in which two atoms are adjacent if they are within a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_o_n_t_a_c_t__m_a_t_r_i_x.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ATOMS<div class="right">the atoms for which you would like to calculate the adjacency matrix<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datcnint">cnint</b> <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">specify the switching function to use between two sets of indistinguishable atoms<i></i></div></div>={RATIONAL R_0=0.355 D_MAX=0.36}
<b name="data/driver.datdfs" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datdfs","data/driver.datdfs","blue")'>dfs</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datdfs">The DFSCLUSTERING action with label <b>dfs</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">dfs</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>vector with length that is equal to the number of rows in the input matrix.  Elements of this vector are equal to the cluster that each node is a part of</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">DFSCLUSTERING<div class="right">Find the connected components of the matrix using the depth first search clustering algorithm. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_d_f_s_c_l_u_s_t_e_r_i_n_g.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">MATRIX<div class="right">the input matrix (can use ARG instead)<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datmat">mat</b>
<br/><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># Cluster size distribution and number of ion clusters/monomers</span>
<span id="data/driver.datnhist_short"><b name="data/driver.datnhist" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datnhist","data/driver.datnhist_shortcut","blue")'>nhist</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datnhist_shortcut">The CLUSTER_DISTRIBUTION action with label <b>nhist</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" style="color:green">CLUSTER_DISTRIBUTION<div class="right">Calculate functions of the distribution of properties in your connected components. This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datnhist");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_l_u_s_t_e_r__d_i_s_t_r_i_b_u_t_i_o_n.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">CLUSTERS<div class="right">the label of the action that does the clustering<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datdfs">dfs</b> <div class="tooltip">HISTOGRAM<div class="right">calculate a discretized histogram of the distribution of values<i></i></div></div>={GAUSSIAN LOWER=-1.5 UPPER=499.5 NBINS=501 NORM}
</span><span id="data/driver.datnhist_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datnhist")'># nhist: CLUSTER_DISTRIBUTION CLUSTERS=dfs HISTOGRAM={GAUSSIAN LOWER=-1.5 UPPER=499.5 NBINS=501 NORM}</span>
<span style="color:blue" class="comment"># as follows (Click the red comment above to revert to the short version of the input):</span>
<b name="data/driver.datnhist_bt1" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datnhist_bt1","data/driver.datnhist_bt1","blue")'>nhist_bt1</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datnhist_bt1">The BETWEEN action with label <b>nhist_bt1</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">nhist_bt1</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of a function that is one if the input falls within a particular range and zero otherwise to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">BETWEEN<div class="right">Use a switching function to determine how many of the input variables are within a certain range. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_b_e_t_w_e_e_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=nhist <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">This keyword is used if you want to employ an alternative to the continuous function defined above<i></i></div></div>={GAUSSIAN LOWER=-1.5 UPPER=-0.5 SMEAR=0.5}

This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datnclust1");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_l_u_s_t_e_r__d_i_s_t_r_i_b_u_t_i_o_n.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">CLUSTERS<div class="right">the label of the action that does the clustering<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datdfs">dfs</b> <div class="tooltip">MORE_THAN<div class="right">calculate the number of variables that are more than a certain target value<i></i></div></div>={GAUSSIAN D_0=0.95 R_0=0.01 D_MAX=0.96}
</span><span id="data/driver.datnclust1_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:

This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datnclust2");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_l_u_s_t_e_r__d_i_s_t_r_i_b_u_t_i_o_n.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">CLUSTERS<div class="right">the label of the action that does the clustering<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datdfs">dfs</b> <div class="tooltip">MORE_THAN<div class="right">calculate the number of variables that are more than a certain target value<i></i></div></div>={GAUSSIAN D_0=1.95 R_0=0.01 D_MAX=1.96}
</span><span id="data/driver.datnclust2_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datnclust2")'># nclust2: CLUSTER_DISTRIBUTION CLUSTERS=dfs MORE_THAN={GAUSSIAN D_0=1.95 R_0=0.01 D_MAX=1.96}</span>

<b name="data/driver.datnclust2_mt" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datnclust2_mt","data/driver.datnclust2_mt","blue")'>nclust2_mt</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datnclust2_mt">The MORE_THAN action with label <b>nclust2_mt</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">nclust2_mt</td><td width="5%"><font color="blue">vector</font></td><td>the vector obtained by doing an element-wise application of a function that is one if the if the input is more than a threshold to the input vectors</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">MORE_THAN<div class="right">Use a switching function to determine how many of the input variables are more than a certain cutoff. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_m_o_r_e__t_h_a_n.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=nclust2 <div class="tooltip">SWITCH<div class="right">This keyword is used if you want to employ an alternative to the continuous swiching function defined above<i></i></div></div>={GAUSSIAN D_0=1.95 R_0=0.01 D_MAX=1.96} 
<b name="data/driver.datnclust2_morethan" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datnclust2_morethan","data/driver.datnclust2_morethan","black")'>nclust2_morethan</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datnclust2_morethan">The SUM action with label <b>nclust2_morethan</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">nclust2_morethan</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the sum of all the elements in the input vector</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">SUM<div class="right">Calculate the sum of the arguments <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_s_u_m.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input to this function<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datnclust2_mt">nclust2_mt</b> <div class="tooltip">PERIODIC<div class="right">if the output of your function is periodic then you should specify the periodicity of the function<i></i></div></div>=NO

<span id="data/driver.datclust0nat_short"><b name="data/driver.datclust0nat" onclick='showPath("data/driver.dat","data/driver.datclust0nat","data/driver.datclust0nat_shortcut","black")'>clust0nat</b><span style="display:none;" id="data/driver.datclust0nat_shortcut">The CLUSTER_NATOMS action with label <b>clust0nat</b> calculates the following quantities:<table  align="center" frame="void" width="95%" cellpadding="5%"><tr><td width="5%"><b> Quantity </b>  </td><td width="5%"><b> Type </b>  </td><td><b> Description </b> </td></tr><tr><td width="5%">clust0nat</td><td width="5%"><font color="black">scalar</font></td><td>the number of atoms in the cluster</td></tr></table></span>: <div class="tooltip" style="color:green">CLUSTER_NATOMS<div class="right">Calculate the number of atoms in the cluster of interest This action is <a class="toggler" href='javascript:;' onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datclust0nat");'>a shortcut</a>. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_c_l_u_s_t_e_r__n_a_t_o_m_s.html">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">CLUSTERS<div class="right">the label of the action that does the clustering<i></i></div></div>=<b name="data/driver.datdfs">dfs</b> <div class="tooltip">CLUSTER<div class="right"> which cluster would you like to look at 1 is the largest cluster, 2 is the second largest, 3 is the the third largest and so on<i></i></div></div>=1
</span><span id="data/driver.datclust0nat_long" style="display:none;"><span style="color:blue" class="comment"># PLUMED interprets the command:
</span><span class="toggler" style="color:red" onclick='toggleDisplay("data/driver.datclust0nat")'># clust0nat: CLUSTER_NATOMS CLUSTERS=dfs CLUSTER=1</span>

<div class="tooltip" style="color:green">PRINT<div class="right">Print quantities to a file. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_p_r_i_n_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=nfree.*,nclust1.*,nclust2.*,<b name="data/driver.datclust0nat">clust0nat.*</b>,<b name="data/driver.datclust1nat">clust1nat.*</b>,<b name="data/driver.datclust2nat">clust2nat</b> <div class="tooltip">FILE<div class="right">the name of the file on which to output these quantities<i></i></div></div>=cluster.dat
<div class="tooltip" style="color:green">PRINT<div class="right">Print quantities to a file. <a href="https://www.plumed.org/doc-master/user-doc/html/_p_r_i_n_t.html" style="color:green">More details</a><i></i></div></div> <div class="tooltip">ARG<div class="right">the input for this action is the scalar output from one or more other actions<i></i></div></div>=nhist <div class="tooltip">FILE<div class="right">the name of the file on which to output these quantities<i></i></div></div>=csd.dat
